atoms.semantic_claim: Scheduled verification preserves typed contract-reader errors for database, malformed storage, and missing scheduler calls.

destinations.snippet: pub enum SchedulerError { ... DatabaseError(String), InvalidStorageData { slot: U256, reason: String }, CallNotFound { call_id: u64 } }
destinations.evidence: The same SchedulerError enum exists byte-for-byte in the v2.2 port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Exercise database failure, malformed slot data, and missing call id reads and assert the specific SchedulerError variant.
atoms.notes: Exact code exists in the v2.2 port at the same path.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: new file: ScheduledConstraintViolation enum and Display impl
atoms.source_snippet: ScheduledConstraintViolation::{CallNotFound, InvalidCallState, IndexOutOfBounds, ExecutionTooEarly, ExecutionTooLate, CallExpired, InvalidSignature, InvalidTarget, InsufficientFunds, AlreadyExecuted, DuplicateInBlock, PredicateFailed, PredicateReverted}
atoms.semantic_claim: Scheduled verification preserves consensus-critical rejection reasons C1-C11 and their Display messages.

destinations.snippet: pub enum ScheduledConstraintViolation { ... PredicateFailed { call_id: u64, predicate: Address }, PredicateReverted { call_id: u64, predicate: Address } }
destinations.evidence: The same violation enum and Display match arms are byte-for-byte present in the v2.2 port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: For each C1-C11 scheduled transaction violation stimulus, assert the verifier emits the matching ScheduledConstraintViolation variant and Display code.
atoms.notes: Exact code exists in the v2.2 port at the same path.
